title: Design a Last-Mile SOP
tagline: Write the protocol for handling "Customer Not Home" scenarios.
scenario: A new grocery delivery app is losing money because drivers are waiting 15 minutes outside apartments when customers don't answer the intercom. They return the groceries, the cold chain breaks, and the food is written off. You need a strict "Customer Not Home" protocol.
brief: Design a Last-Mile SOP that balances customer service with driver efficiency and cold-chain compliance.
deliverables: The step-by-step protocol for the driver when a customer does not answer
The exact time limit the driver is allowed to wait
The script the driver must use when calling the customer
The policy on what to do with the groceries if the delivery fails (e.g., leave at gate vs. return to dark store)
submissionGuidance: Drivers need binary rules. "Wait a reasonable amount of time" is bad ops. "Wait exactly 5 minutes, call twice, then swipe Failed" is good ops.

rubric.label: Binary Rules
rubric.description: Are the time limits and steps strict and unambiguous?

rubric.label: Driver Efficiency
rubric.description: Does the SOP protect the driver's time?

rubric.label: Cold-Chain Protection
rubric.description: Is the return/abandon policy clear regarding perishable goods?
